title: eryngium floribundum
intro: Eryngium floribundum, also known as the Mexican gold poppy or butterfly flower, is a flowering plant species in the carrot family, characterized by its bright yellow, orange, or red flowers and fern-like foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Eryngium floribundum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its dried flowers and leaves can be used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
